ddls:
CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project */,
  "project_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project manager responsible for the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projects_project_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_manager_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_cases" (
  "test_case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test case */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project the test case belongs to */,
  "module_name" TEXT /* Name of the module the test case belongs to */,
  "test_case_name" TEXT /* Name of the test case */,
  "validation_steps" TEXT /* Validation steps for the test case */,
  "expected_result" TEXT /* Expected result of the test case */,
  "actual_result" TEXT /* Actual result of the test case */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Status of the validation (e.g., PASS, FAIL) */,
  "execution_time" TEXT /* Execution time of the test case */,
  "test_case_status" TEXT /* Status of the test case (e.g., PASS, FAIL, PENDING) */,
  "test_case_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the test case */,
  "test_case_type" TEXT /* Type of the test case (e.g., Functional, Performance, Security) */,
  "test_case_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the test case (e.g., High, Medium, Low) */,
  "test_case_created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the test case */,
  "test_case_created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case was created */,
  "test_case_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the test case */,
  "test_case_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the test case was last updated */,
  "test_case_assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user to whom the test case is assigned */,
  "test_case_comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes related to the test case */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_project_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_id") REFERENCES projects ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_test_case_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_test_case_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_validation_steps FOREIGN KEY ("validation_steps") REFERENCES validation_steps ("validation_step_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_cases_test_case_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("test_case_updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "validation_steps" (
  "validation_step_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation step */,
  "validation_step_name" TEXT /* Name of the validation step */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the validation step */,
  "step_order" INTEGER /* Order in which the validation steps should be executed */,
  "step_duration" TEXT /* Estimated or actual duration of the validation step */,
  "step_status" TEXT /* Status of the validation step (e.g., PASS, FAIL, PENDING) */,
  "step_comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes related to the validation step */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_step_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user (hashed and salted) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., Active, Inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the permission was assigned to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
